subject: A large arthropod creature lurking inside a stone culvert tunnel
description: A massive crustacean or insectoid creature fills the interior of a stone-lined tunnel opening. Its body is dark and segmented, with prominent orange-tipped curved horns or spines radiating from its bulk. The culvert arch frames the creature, with a green-lit watery or mossy interior behind it. The creature crouches low and forward, as if about to surge out of the tunnel. A long dark tail or appendage curves away to the right. The setting is dank and enclosed.
foreground: Dark segmented arthropod with orange-tipped horns, filling a culvert opening
background: Stone culvert arch, mossy green interior
emotion: predatory anticipation; the creature is coiled to strike
